parsed_review.summary: CP00-699 is a descriptor-only RP23 "External Integrations II" finance-integrations pack (risk B, 40 units, RP23.P02.M04.S19-RP23.P02.M06.S14, handoff to CP00-700/RP23.P02.M06.S15). The pack-level production_ready claim is appropriately scoped and supported. DIRECTLY VERIFIED via Read/Grep: (1) CP699 binding (registry.js:86-101) matches the pack baseline exactly (risk_class, unit_count, first/last unit, next_pack/subphase); (2) deliverable distribution is exact (test 8, contract 2, implementation 17, security_audit 4, ui 5, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 2 = 40) and micro-title split (Secondary Workflow Slice 4, Permission And Audit Binding 22, Synthetic Fixture Set 14 = 40), confirmed by hand against the baseline included_units; (3) comprehensive NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:103) plus zero sensitive flags set true anywhere in the 833KB contract (0 matches for runtime-opened/credential/financial-payload/raw-payload/cross-tenant/permission-decision-detail/audit-event-body/writes-product-state=true; 0 descriptor_only:false; 0 runtime_execution:true across 2026 descriptor rows); (4) authority boundary keeps Claude read_only and non-final with human approval required for runtime opening and local validation not claiming enterprise trust (service.js:104-111, enforced by validators.js:8); (5) Hermes packets are deterministic descriptor-only review/receipt artifacts with emits_runtime_receipt=false and writes_product_state=false (service.js:129) — not product runtime; (6) no credential/real-data leaks (all "credential"/"secret" hits are negative attestations/guards); (7) production_ready is honestly false and consistent across manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication, and claude-review-result, all in pending_review state awaiting exactly this receipt; the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification token, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim. REPORTED EVIDENCE (not re-run under this read-only review): command-evidence.json shows the 23-command validation matrix plus npm test/build all exit_code 0, and readiness-report.json shows the auth preflight passed with valid JSON (1455 bytes) — no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, or tool-call-shaped output is being counted as review evidence. Claude remains the independent read-only reviewer, not final approver. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note about generator labeling. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block this CP closeout or this CP-level goal closeout. Enterprise-trust/runtime opening remains a human-approval boundary outside this receipt.

findings.title: Section micro_titles are ledger labels decoupled from the standard service-workflow row template
findings.details: In the CP699 requirements (registry.js:815-895), micro-phase RP23.P02.M05 is labeled micro_title 'Permission And Audit Binding' and RP23.P02.M06 'Synthetic Fixture Set', but both required_section_rows carry the standard service-workflow template (Service entrypoint contract, Request normalization, Tenant/Matter/Permission/Audit prechecks, Primary/Secondary paths, ... Review-required routing) rather than permission/audit- or fixture-specific rows. This is deterministic behavior of the weighted-implementation-ledger generator and is consistent with CP00-697/CP00-698. It does not affect descriptor integrity: each row's ROW_EXTRAS metadata (service.js:18-62) is individually correct and runtime-closed (e.g., permission_precheck.evaluates_runtime_permission=false, fixture_model.real_financial_data_loaded=false), and validators enforce structural row-count/no-leak consistency (validators.js:7-8) independent of the section label. No real data or runtime is involved.
findings.recommendation: No action required for CP00-699 closeout. Optionally document in the generator that micro_title is a ledger label decoupled from the row template, to prevent reviewer confusion in future RP23 packs.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational_accepted — consistent with the established descriptor-only generator pattern across CP00-694..CP00-698; does not weaken the no-write/no-real-data boundaries or the descriptor-scoped production_ready flag, and does not block pack or goal closeout.
